Kandovan’s cone-shaped homes are carved from volcanic rock—and people still live inside them. - Fun Facts
A recycling worker opened an old TV and found over $100,000—then the money was returned to the man who hid it decades earlier. - Fun Facts
A rival cut off Takeda Shingen’s salt. Uesugi Kenshin sent him salt anyway—saying wars are won with swords, not salt. - Fun Facts
Every other planet in the solar system can fit between Earth and the Moon — with roughly 4,400 km to spare. - Fun Facts
Walmart paid $65 million because it refused to let its California cashiers sit down while working. - Fun Facts

During a war between France and England in 1697, Louis XIV ordered the release of a captured English engineer who had been building a lighthouse. His reasoning: "France is at war with England, not with humanity." - Fun Facts
In Portugal, it's illegal for your employer to contact you outside of working hours — companies face fines of nearly €10,000 per violation. - Fun Facts

In 1978, Richard Branson tried to impress his girlfriend by pretending to buy a Caribbean island listed at $6 million. He jokingly offered $100,000 and was thrown off the island — but a year later, with no other buyers, the desperate owner accepted just $180,000. - Fun Facts
In 1869–1870, boosters called Washington vulnerable and pitched St. Louis as the nation’s natural center, even hosting a Capital Removal convention. - Fun Facts

In 1930, six anonymous Chicago businessmen formed a secret vigilante organization to take on the city's rampant crime. They investigated bombings, kidnappings, and bank robberies — and Al Capone himself said they were responsible for bringing him down. - Fun Facts
About 2% of people worldwide can hear a mysterious low-frequency humming sound known as "The Hum" — and despite decades of research, no one has been able to fully explain it. - Fun Facts
A group of kids learned sign language just so they could wish their deaf janitor a happy birthday. - Fun Facts
